text:
CT ABDOMEN AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
SURVEILLANCE STUDY

CLINICAL INDICATION: 53-year-old female with history of FIGO stage IIIC high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma, bilateral. Status post primary debulking surgery (total abdominal hysterectomy, bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy, infracolic omentectomy, peritoneal stripping, right hemidiaphragmatic peritonectomy) achieving R0 resection. Subsequently completed 6 cycles of adjuvant carbo/PTX with CA-125 normalization to 11 U/mL. Currently on nira maintenance therapy, dose-reduced to 100 mg daily secondary to prior Grade 3 thrombocytopenia. Most recent surveillance CT approximately 6 months prior showed no evidence of disease. Present study obtained for 12-month post-chemotherapy surveillance. Known germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant. CA-125 at time of scan: 8 U/mL.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the abdomen and pelvis was performed following the administration of 100 mL of Omnipaque 350 intravenous contrast material via antecubital vein, acquired in the portal venous phase. Coronal and sagittal multiplanar reformations generated and reviewed. Oral contrast was not administered per departmental protocol for oncologic surveillance.

---

FINDINGS:

PERITONEUM AND MESENTERY:
No peritoneal nodularity or soft tissue thickening identified along the parietal or visceral peritoneal surfaces. The mesentery appears homogeneous without nodular or stellate soft tissue infiltration. No evidence of omental recurrence; the resected omental bed demonstrates expected post-surgical fatty fibrofatty changes without discrete soft tissue nodularity. The paracolic gutters - right and left - are clear, without fluid loculation or soft tissue implants. No abnormal peritoneal enhancement is identified.

ABDOMEN - SOLID ORGANS:
Liver: The liver is normal in size with smooth contour and homogeneous parenchymal attenuation. No hepatic lesions are identified. The hepatic dome is carefully evaluated given history of prior surface implants stripped at surgery; the hepatic dome peritoneum is smooth without focal nodular thickening or enhancement. Portal and hepatic veins are patent. No intrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation.

Spleen: Spleen is normal in size and homogeneity. No focal splenic lesions.

Pancreas: Pancreatic parenchyma is normal in caliber and attenuation. Pancreatic duct is not dilated. No peripancreatic fluid or soft tissue change.

Adrenal glands: Bilateral adrenal glands appear unremarkable without nodularity or mass.

Kidneys: Both kidneys are present and of normal size with smooth renal cortical contour. No hydronephrosis. No cortical masses. Normal contrast excretion bilaterally. No perinephric stranding.

GALLBLADDER AND BILIARY SYSTEM:
Gallbladder is present with thin walls, no gallstones or pericholecystic fluid. Common bile duct measures 4 mm, within normal limits.

GASTROINTESTINAL TRACT:
Visualized bowel loops throughout the abdomen and pelvis are normal in caliber without evidence of obstruction or wall thickening. No intraluminal mass or abnormal mucosal enhancement. Small bowel loops are distributed normally. The appendix was removed at prior surgery and is absent as expected.

PELVIS:
Uterus and bilateral ovaries are absent, consistent with prior total abdominal hysterectomy and bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy. The vaginal cuff is surgically closed with expected post-operative fibrosis and no evidence of cuff recurrence or soft tissue mass at the vaginal apex. The bladder is unremarkable in appearance with smooth walls and no intraluminal filling defect. Rectosigmoid colon is unremarkable. The presacral space is clear without soft tissue density. No abnormal soft tissue in the pelvis. No pelvic free fluid.

LYMPH NODES:
No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes are identified in the retroperitoneum, mesentery, or pelvis. Specifically, no significant lymph nodes are seen in the para-aortic, paracaval, common iliac, external iliac, or obturator distributions. Small lymph nodes measuring up to 7 mm in the right inguinal region are noted, appearing reactive without concerning morphology.

VASCULAR STRUCTURES:
Aorta is normal in caliber without aneurysmal change. Inferior vena cava is patent. No venous thrombosis identified in the visualized pelvic vasculature.

BONY STRUCTURES:
Visualized osseous structures including the lumbar spine, sacrum, pelvis, and proximal femora demonstrate no lytic or blastic lesions to suggest osseous metastatic involvement. No fracture.

SOFT TISSUES AND BODY WALL:
Abdominal wall demonstrates a well-healed midline laparotomy scar with expected fibrotic changes. No hernia identified at the surgical incision site. No soft tissue masses in the abdominal or pelvic wall.

LIMITATIONS: Examination is limited to the abdomen and pelvis; chest not imaged on this study. Small-volume peritoneal disease below the resolution of CT cannot be fully excluded.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. No radiographic evidence of disease recurrence. The peritoneal surfaces, mesentery, omental bed, and hepatic dome - all sites of known prior disease involvement - appear clear without nodular implants, thickening, or abnormal enhancement.

2. The surgical bed is well-defined with expected post-operative changes following hysterectomy, bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy, and omentectomy. No mass or soft tissue nodularity at the vaginal cuff.

3. No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes in the retroperitoneum or pelvis.

4. Liver parenchyma normal without focal lesions; hepatic dome peritoneum is smooth.

5. No ascites.

Overall, the abdomen and pelvis appear clear at this 12-month post-chemotherapy surveillance imaging, consistent with sustained radiographic remission in the setting of ongoing Zejula maintenance therapy.

Dictated and electronically signed by: Attending Radiologist, Abdominal Imaging Division
